title: blephariglottis lacera var. terrae-novae
intro:
Blephariglottis lacera var. terrae-novae, commonly known as Yellow Goodyera, is a type of orchid species native to the island of Newfoundland and Labrador in Canada.

This plant is known for its potential health benefits, including the ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health due to its vasodilatory properties.

The therapeutic actions of Yellow Goodyera are attributed to its bioactive constituents, which include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds that may exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects.

However, more research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits and therapeutic actions of this plant, and it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment without consulting a healthcare professional.
side_effects: Blephariglottis lacera var. terrae-novae can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and vomiting, as well as allergic reactions like hives and respiratory distress in rare cases. Long-term consumption may lead to liver damage and increased risk of bleeding due to its potential interactions with blood-thinning medications. Using the plant in excessive amounts can also result in headaches, dizziness, and altered mental state. Furthermore, its improper use during pregnancy or breastfeeding may cause harm to the fetus or baby, highlighting the need for caution and medical supervision.
